message:
feat(engine): support trailing-slash directory specifiers in require() and add exhaustive module resolution test matrix (~80 new tests, +6293 lines)

Module resolution change (baseline_interpreter.rs):
- resolve_require_specifier now detects specifiers ending with '/' and
  passes a force_directory flag to resolve_require_candidate. When set,
  the resolver skips all file-matching logic (exact file, extensionless
  .cjs/.js/.mjs probes) and goes directly to directory index resolution.
  This matches Node.js behavior where require('./pkg/') must resolve to
  pkg/index.{cjs,js,mjs}, never to a file named 'pkg'.
- resolve_require_candidate refactored to accept force_directory parameter.
  The extension-probe-as-directory fallback (trying ./foo/index.* when
  ./foo has no extension) is also gated behind !force_directory to prevent
  double-probing when the trailing slash already forced directory mode.

Exhaustive module resolution test matrix (+6293 lines, ~80 new tests):
- ESM namespace caching: require_module_caches_esm_namespace_object
  verifies that repeated require() of the same .mjs returns the identical
  namespace object (reference equality).
- Relative path resolution from CJS entries: tests verify that require()
  resolves relative to the requiring file, not the module root. Both
  same-directory and cross-directory scenarios with conflicting file names.
- Extensionless resolution priority: .cjs is preferred over .js, .js over
  .mjs, matching Node.js CJS resolver semantics.
- Parent-relative paths (../): resolution from nested CJS files correctly
  traverses up to parent directories, with extension priority preserved.
- Directory resolution: both explicit trailing-slash (require('./pkg/'))
  and implicit directory probing (require('./pkg') when pkg/ exists).
  Index file priority: index.cjs > index.js > index.mjs.
- Trailing-slash vs file neighbor: require('./pkg/') must resolve to
  pkg/index.* even when a file named 'pkg.cjs' exists alongside the
  directory. Without trailing slash, the file wins.
- Nested require chains: inner CJS modules that require() their own
  relative dependencies verify that __dirname/__filename bindings and
  relative path resolution operate from the inner module's location.
- Combined edge cases: nested parent-relative directory trailing-slash
  resolution with extension priority, explicit index file specifiers,
  and cross-directory extension preference verification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
